_patch_meta.description: Level 1 Tier C audit findings — Phase B quality flags on 4 groups and Session B pointer for group 4986-001 (salvation vocabulary in debauchery registry).

set.notes: QA-REVIEW (Level 1 Tier C, 2026-04-06): Description 'The Sinai/Mosaic covenant — bilateral covenant of law and obedience' is slightly externalised. Anchor Deu 7:9 holds the inner dimension (love, fear, faithfulness) but the description foregrounds the structural/legal covenant frame. 76 related verses include external covenant-making contexts. Phase B should verify whether the inner-being engagement (the human response of love, fear, and obedience) is sufficiently characterised and distinct from purely structural covenant uses.

operations.description: QA-REVIEW flag — covenant 765-002: description slightly externalised

set.notes: QA-REVIEW (Level 1 Tier C, 2026-04-06): Description 'Falsehood as the inner orientation of the wicked — lying tongue and lying heart' reaches beyond what the anchor verse explicitly states. Anchor Pro 12:22 names the external expression (lying lips are an abomination) and implies the inner orientation. The inner-orientation claim is accurate but inferential. Phase B should verify that the inner orientation claim is directly supported across the 73-verse set rather than resting on implication from the anchor alone.

operations.description: QA-REVIEW flag — deceit 784-001: description reaches beyond anchor

set.notes: QA-REVIEW (Level 1 Tier C, 2026-04-06): Anchor Jer 31:33 (I will write my law on their hearts) is excellent — the most inner-being covenant text in the corpus. But 66 related verses include covenant-making contexts (Gen 15:18, Gen 21:27-32) that are primarily external acts. Description 'Divine covenant initiated by God — the relational framework of his commitment' holds at a high level but may not be specific enough to distinguish inner-being uses from structural covenant uses. Phase B should verify that 'relational framework' is adequately precise.

operations.description: QA-REVIEW flag — covenant 3304-001: related verse set may include external uses

set.notes: QA-BROAD (Level 1 Tier C, 2026-04-06): Description 'Ya.tav as moral goodness and doing good: inner moral orientation' conflates two distinguishable uses. Anchors Jer 13:23 (can you do good who are accustomed to do evil?) and Jer 32:41 (I will rejoice in doing them good, with all my heart and soul) name the inner moral capacity and divine inner motivation respectively. But many related verses (Deu 4:40, 5:16, 5:29, 6:3, 6:18 — that it may go well with you) express covenantal wellbeing/prosperity, not inner moral orientation. Phase B should assess whether these two uses warrant separation into distinct groups or whether a refined description can hold both.

operations.description: QA-BROAD flag — gladness 632-002: conflates moral orientation with covenantal wellbeing

record.finding: Group 4986-001 in debauchery (#39, C12) contains 57 verses of salvation vocabulary (sōzō/sōtēria), anchored at Eph 2:8 ('by grace you have been saved through faith'). Related verses span the NT salvation corpus: Mat 10:22, 19:25, 24:13, Mar 16:16, Luk 8:12, Joh 3:17, Act 2:21, 4:12, and more. This group is structurally unexpected in the debauchery registry but was placed there on the basis of the verse evidence — salvation vocabulary appears in debauchery-adjacent contexts. Session B analyst should assess the connection: is salvation specifically named as the remedy for debauchery/licentiousness in these contexts, does the term appear because the debauchery file included a broad NT lexical sweep, or is there a distinct theological pattern linking salvation and debauchery vocabulary in Scripture? The answer has implications for how the inner-being dimension of salvation is characterised within this registry. This is also a Session D synthesis candidate — the relationship between salvation and debauchery vocabulary across the programme.

operations.description: Session B finding — debauchery (#39): salvation vocabulary in 4986-001 requires analytical assessment
